page: Microsoft Azure D4_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv4 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512F and AVX512_VNNI. This configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on remote storage solutions.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside weaker multi-core and memory throughput. The single-core performance is rated as average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1435.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1215.0. However, multi-core scaling is limited, as shown by a weak stress-ng multi-core score of 3739.96 and a poor bzip3 multi-threaded decompression rate of 17.39 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, registering at 26.1 GB/sec for uncached reads and 21.2 GB/sec for uncached writes. For lightweight workloads, the server manages 98.14 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M LLM model, though prompt processing remains in the lower tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4_v4 offers a standard general-purpose resource density without the premium cost of specialized memory or storage-optimized hardware. Because it lacks local NVMe storage and high-speed memory bandwidth, it is less suitable for heavy database operations or high-throughput web serving. Instead, this virtual machine is best suited for general-purpose workloads, light application hosting, single-threaded processing tasks, and basic development or testing environments where balanced CPU-to-memory ratios are required without extreme performance demands.
description: Microsoft Azure D4_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyperthreading, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1215.0, but weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, which registers at 26.1 GB/sec for uncached reads. Qualitatively, this server offers a balanced resource profile for standard workloads without the overhead of specialized hardware. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, and development environments that do not require high-throughput multi-threaded processing or local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering balanced resources for standard workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and moderate single-core performance for standard computing workloads.
